metadata.title: Naji Najari — Senior AI Engineer | Multi-Agent Systems, RAG, LLMOps
metadata.description: Senior AI Engineer with a PhD in Machine Learning. Building multi-agent AI systems in production: LangGraph, RAG, Google ADK, LLMOps. Open to freelance missions.
metadata.og_description: Senior AI Engineer with a PhD in ML. Building multi-agent AI systems, RAG pipelines, and LLM-powered products in production.
metadata.twitter_description: Senior AI Engineer with a PhD in ML. AI Agents, RAG, LLMOps. Open to freelance.

hero.name: Naji Najari, Ph.D.
hero.description: Building multi-agent platforms, RAG pipelines, and LLM-powered products at Brevo. PhD in unsupervised anomaly detection.

about.title: About
about.subtitle: Senior AI Engineer with a PhD in Machine Learning. I help companies ship AI agents, RAG systems, and LLM-powered products to production.
about.agents_desc: I design multi-agent systems that go beyond chatbots. Agents that call your APIs, query your data, and execute real business logic. Built to run 24/7, not just during a demo.
about.rag_desc: I build retrieval systems that give your users accurate answers from your own documents. Hybrid search, reranking, chunking strategies, and proper evaluation to avoid hallucinations.
about.eval_desc: I set up tracing, LLM-as-a-judge scoring, and human review pipelines so you can measure your AI quality and improve it continuously.
about.finetune_desc: When a generic model isn't enough, I fine-tune open-source LLMs on your data. Custom classifiers, domain-specific generation, multilingual NLP. From training to serving.

experience.title: Experience
brevo_bullets: Built multiple production agents within a multi-agent AI platform used by end-users and internal teams: documentation Q&A (RAG), CRM contact management, campaign analytics and recommendation | LangChain, LangGraph, Google ADK
Exposed Brevo's public APIs as MCP servers, enabling developers to use Brevo tools directly from AI coding assistants (Claude, Cursor) | FastMCP, FastAPI
Implemented production-grade RAG pipelines for real-time customer query answering | Ragflow, LangChain
Integrated Langfuse for end-to-end LLM tracing and monitoring; built LLM-as-a-judge evaluation with human-in-the-loop annotation
Fine-tuned BERT for multilingual spam and fraud detection | HuggingFace Transformers, CI/CD
Trained and deployed ML audience recommendation models | ZenML, MLOps, MLFlow
forvia_bullets: Led a cross-functional team of 6-8 (data scientists, software engineers, front-end developers) delivering ML solutions for manufacturing plants at world's 4th largest automotive supplier
Built predictive maintenance models: process parameter optimization, time series forecasting, anomaly detection
Applied LLMs for advanced text analysis on manufacturing documentation and reports
Recognized internally as Machine Learning Expert
orange_bullets: Trained and deployed robust unsupervised anomaly detection models for real-time network traffic monitoring | PyTorch, PySpark, Docker
Published 4 papers at top ML conferences: ECML-PKDD, IEEE COINS, AINA, SINConf
Filed 2 international patents (INPI) for IoT anomaly detection and network traffic fingerprinting

projects.title: Projects
projects.career_copilot_title: Career Copilot · Multi-agent CV × JD analyzer
projects.career_copilot_desc: Multi-agent app on Google ADK v2: parses a CV and a job description in parallel, then routes to a recruiter flow (fit verdict + LinkedIn outreach citing a real CV achievement) or a candidate flow (live company research via Tavily MCP + tailored interview prep). Typed Pydantic outputs, Langfuse tracing.

career_copilot_page.meta_title: Career Copilot · AI for recruiters and candidates | Naji Najari
career_copilot_page.meta_description: Career Copilot reads any CV against any job ad in seconds. Recruiters get a fit verdict and ready-to-send LinkedIn outreach citing real CV achievements. Candidates get live company research and tailored interview prep. Free demo.
career_copilot_page.title: Career Copilot
career_copilot_page.tagline: Drop in a CV and a job ad. Recruiters get a fit verdict and outreach worth sending. Candidates get a company brief and tailored interview prep.
career_copilot_page.agentic_comment: The graph encodes a deliberate separation between deterministic control flow and LLM-driven generation. Routing and synchronization stay in pure Python; only generation crosses an LLM boundary, and every output is constrained by a Pydantic schema before it leaves the node.

agentic_wiring_bullets.prefix: Control flow stays out of the LLMs
sub: Two FunctionNode routers (mode_router, verdict_router) decide branches in pure Python on already-typed state.
JoinNodes wait for parallel branches to complete before firing downstream.
No LLM ever decides which branch fires next, which removes a whole class of failure modes from the critical path.

agentic_wiring_bullets.prefix: Eight specialised agents, one schema each
sub: Each agent declares its own output_schema: ParsedCV, ParsedJD, FitVerdict, OutreachDraft, GapReport, CompanyIntelligence, CVOptimizationBundle, InterviewPrepBundle.

agentic_wiring_bullets.prefix: Two branch topologies on the same parsed inputs
sub: Recruiter branch: linear chain with one conditional split on the fit verdict (fit / borderline → outreach, no_fit → gap report).
Candidate branch: research and CV positioning fan out in parallel, synchronize through a JoinNode, then feed interview prep.
The candidate-side parallelism is safe because the two passes have no data dependency on each other.

agentic_wiring_bullets.prefix: One escape hatch for tool use
sub: ADK currently disallows combining output_schema with tools on gpt-5.4-mini.
The Research Agent uses the Tavily MCP toolset and emits its CompanyIntelligence payload as a JSON string.
The handler validates it with model_validate_json at the API boundary, preserving the typed-state invariant at the edge.

career_copilot_page.design_title: The agentic graph above only ships if it is exposed through a stateless API and reachable from a UI. Two clean layers do that without leaking complexity into the graph.

design_backend_bullets.desc: two stateless POST endpoints (/v1/analyze, /v1/extract-pdf). Auto-generated OpenAPI schema and native Pydantic integration.
FastAPI plus the ADK async runner so parallel branches in the graph actually run in parallel.
single Dockerfile, uv lockfile. Deploys identically on Cloud Run, Fly.io, or HuggingFace Spaces.

design_frontend_bullets.desc: collect inputs, POST to /v1/analyze, render the typed response. All business logic stays in the backend graph.

design_frontend_bullets.prefix: TanStack Query for the analyze call
design_frontend_bullets.desc: useMutation drives the flow; Sonner toasts surface the structured 502 / 422 errors from the backend.
a single analyzeInputSchema for both modes. Paste text or upload a PDF (routed through /v1/extract-pdf first).

career_copilot_page.design_intro_unused: A browser hits a Next.js 15 / React 19 frontend. The frontend posts CV + JD to a stateless FastAPI endpoint. The backend kicks off a Google ADK v2 Workflow graph, awaits the typed response, and returns it. Nothing is shared between requests, so every layer scales horizontally without coordination, and any instance can serve any request.
career_copilot_page.agentic_intro: The orchestration is one Workflow definition. Edges describe parallel branches (tuples), conditional routing (FunctionNode + dict mapping), and fan-in synchronization (JoinNodes). Determinism comes from the typed state passed between nodes; nothing relies on parsing strings, nothing flows untyped across an LLM boundary.

agentic_decisions.title: Parallel CV / JD parsing
agentic_decisions.desc: Independent extractions, fired concurrently before the mode decision is even taken. Halves the preprocessing tail latency on every request.

agentic_decisions.title: JoinNodes for fan-in sync
agentic_decisions.desc: Without an explicit join, the next node would fire once per incoming branch. JoinNodes wait for all upstream results and pass a unified state forward.

agentic_decisions.title: FunctionNode routers, not LLMs
agentic_decisions.desc: Mode and verdict routing are deterministic decisions on already-typed state. No LLM tax, no hallucination risk on the code path that decides which branch fires.

agentic_decisions.title: Typed contracts at every boundary
agentic_decisions.desc: Each LLM agent declares a Pydantic output_schema enforced by ADK. Invalid output fails the node fast and loud instead of corrupting state in a downstream agent.

agentic_decisions.title: Research Agent escapes the schema
agentic_decisions.desc: ADK cannot combine output_schema with tools on gpt-5.4-mini. Research Agent uses Tavily MCP tools and emits a JSON string the API handler validates with model_validate_json at the boundary.

agentic_decisions.title: Reasoning effort, dialled per agent
agentic_decisions.desc: Default low reasoning effort to keep cost predictable. The Outreach Writer is the only agent dialled up to medium, where copy quality moves the needle.

career_copilot_page.problem_title: Google ADK v2 shipped its graph workflow API recently. I wanted to push it end-to-end on a real use case, in the spirit of LangGraph.

problem_bullets.desc: nodes, edges, parallel branches, conditional routing, and sync points are first-class.
each node can be unit-tested, swapped, or traced on its own.
contracts at every step instead of free-form prompt parsing.
you know upfront which steps run, in what order, and what model serves each one.

career_copilot_page.modes_title: Upload a CV and a job description. An agent pipeline runs the full analysis and produces an output tailored to whichever side of the hiring loop is asking, recruiter or candidate.

modes_bullets.desc: the CV and the job description are parsed in parallel into typed objects (skills, years of experience, achievements, required vs preferred skills, seniority). All branching happens after parsing.

scores how well the candidate fits the role (fit, borderline, or no fit), then either drafts a LinkedIn outreach quoting a real achievement from the CV, or explains the gaps and suggests adjacent roles.
live company research via Tavily MCP (funding, culture, recent news), CV positioning suggestions tailored to the job ad, and an interview prep bundle with probable questions and talking points.

modes_graph_bullets.desc: both flows reuse the same parsed CV and JD, then produce completely different artefacts. A single big-prompt agent would need a sprawling conditional prompt; a graph makes the split natural.
parsing the CV and the JD concurrently, then on the candidate side running research and CV positioning in parallel. A linear chain would force these steps to run one after the other.
routing on the fit verdict (fit or borderline → outreach, no_fit → gap explainer) cannot depend on an LLM re-parsing its own output. A FunctionNode router decides in pure Python on a typed verdict.
small enough to ship as a side project, real enough to expose failure modes, rich enough to need every one of the affordances above.

career_copilot_page.modes_intro: Pick who you are. The same CV and job ad lead to very different answers.
career_copilot_page.recruiter_desc: Know in seconds whether the candidate fits the role. If they do, get a LinkedIn message that does not read like a template.
recruiter_bullets: A clear fit verdict (fit, borderline, or no fit) with confidence and matched evidence from the CV
A LinkedIn outreach draft that quotes a real achievement from the candidate's CV
When the fit isn't there: a plain explanation of the gaps and adjacent roles worth pursuing
career_copilot_page.candidate_desc: Walk into the interview already knowing the company and what they're likely to ask you.
candidate_bullets: Live company research: funding, culture, Glassdoor signals, recent news
Targeted CV improvements matched to the job ad, without inventing experience
Interview prep tailored to the role: probable questions, talking points, smart questions to ask back
career_copilot_page.architecture_intro: Three tiers. A Next.js 15 / React 19 frontend handles uploads, mode selection and live results. A FastAPI backend exposes two stateless endpoints and orchestrates a Google ADK v2 graph workflow. The graph runs eight LLM agents on OpenAI gpt-5.4-mini via LiteLlm, with parallel branches synchronized by JoinNodes and external tooling reached through MCP.
career_copilot_page.tier_frontend_desc: Next.js 15 (App Router, RSC), React 19, TanStack Query for request state, Zod for client-side validation, Radix UI primitives, Tailwind v4.
career_copilot_page.tier_backend_desc: FastAPI with two stateless endpoints, async I/O end-to-end, structured logging middleware, typed error contracts (502 / 500 / 415 / 413 / 422).
career_copilot_page.tier_orchestration_desc: Google ADK v2 Workflow graph: parallel parsers, JoinNodes for fan-in sync, FunctionNode routers, sub-agents with Pydantic output schemas, MCP toolset for Tavily.
career_copilot_page.architecture_legend: Blue: LLM agents · Green: pure-Python nodes (routers, join) · Orange (dashed): external tools via MCP · Grey: HTTP I/O.
career_copilot_page.graph_intro: The whole orchestration is declared in one Workflow definition. Edges describe the topology: parallel branches use tuples, routers map symbolic outcomes to next nodes, JoinNodes synchronize fan-in. Determinism comes from the typed state passed between nodes; nothing relies on string parsing.
career_copilot_page.agents_intro: Eight LLM agents, two FunctionNode routers, two JoinNodes. Every agent runs OpenAI gpt-5.4-mini via LiteLlm with low reasoning effort by default. The Outreach Writer escalates to medium reasoning for sharper, less generic copy. Each agent's output is a Pydantic schema enforced by ADK's output_schema, except the Research Agent which uses Tavily tools and validates its JSON output at the API boundary.
career_copilot_page.agent_cv_parser_desc: Extracts skills, years of experience, achievements and languages into a typed ParsedCV.
career_copilot_page.agent_jd_parser_desc: Extracts title, company, required and preferred skills, seniority and agency hints into a ParsedJD.
career_copilot_page.agent_fit_analyzer_desc: Compares ParsedCV against ParsedJD and emits a FitVerdict with calibrated confidence, matched strengths and gaps.
career_copilot_page.agent_outreach_writer_desc: Writes a LinkedIn outreach draft that cites one specific CV achievement verbatim. Medium reasoning effort.
career_copilot_page.agent_gap_explainer_desc: On no_fit, explains the gaps in plain language and suggests adjacent roles worth pursuing.
career_copilot_page.agent_research_desc: Calls Tavily search and extract via MCP to gather company intelligence: funding, culture, Glassdoor signals.
career_copilot_page.agent_cv_optimizer_desc: Suggests targeted CV edits to better match the JD, without lying about the candidate's experience.
career_copilot_page.agent_interview_prep_desc: Builds an interview prep bundle: probable questions, talking points and smart reverse questions.
career_copilot_page.api_intro: Two stateless endpoints, both POST. Every response shape is a Pydantic model. Clients get a strict OpenAPI schema and FastAPI auto-generates the docs at /docs.
career_copilot_page.api_analyze_desc: Runs the full graph against a CV + JD pair and returns a typed response discriminated by mode. Errors propagate as structured HTTPExceptions with actionable messages.
career_copilot_page.api_pdf_desc: Uploads a PDF, validates content-type and size, returns concatenated page text. Rejects scanned PDFs with no extractable text.
career_copilot_page.scale_intro: Built to scale horizontally on serverless platforms (Cloud Run, HF Spaces) without state coupling between requests. Every design decision favors predictable cost and latency over feature breadth.

scale_bullets.title: Stateless workers
scale_bullets.desc: No in-memory session, no per-instance cache. Every request carries its own state. Drops trivially behind any load balancer or autoscaler.

scale_bullets.title: Async end-to-end
scale_bullets.desc: FastAPI + httpx + ADK async runner. Parallel CV/JD parsing halves the LLM-bound tail latency on every request.

scale_bullets.title: Cost-aware reasoning
scale_bullets.desc: Default low reasoning effort on gpt-5.4-mini. Only the Outreach Writer escalates to medium where copy quality moves the needle.

scale_bullets.title: Typed contracts everywhere
scale_bullets.desc: Pydantic schemas at every node boundary and at the API edge. Invalid LLM output fails fast and loud, not silently with a corrupted downstream state.

scale_bullets.title: Strict input validation
scale_bullets.desc: PDF endpoint enforces content-type and a 10 MB cap. Empty or scanned-only PDFs return 422 with an actionable message instead of a generic 500.

scale_bullets.title: Container-ready
scale_bullets.desc: Dockerfile + uv lockfile. Deploys to Cloud Run, Fly.io, or HF Spaces. Environment-driven config, no hardcoded secrets, no init scripts.

career_copilot_page.obs_title: Every request, traced end-to-end with Langfuse.
career_copilot_page.obs_intro: An LLM system you cannot trace is a system you cannot fix. Every /v1/analyze call opens a parent agent observation in Langfuse. Sub-agent and tool calls nest under it as child spans, with inputs, outputs, latency and token counts captured. Mode, model, version and input sizes are propagated as trace attributes for fast filtering in the Langfuse UI.

obs_bullets.title: Per-request span tree
obs_bullets.desc: The /v1/analyze handler opens a parent observation typed as 'agent'. ADK runs each sub-agent and tool call as a nested span under it. Every input, output, latency and token count is captured automatically.

obs_bullets.title: Trace attributes for filtering
obs_bullets.desc: propagate_attributes injects mode, model, version, cv_chars and jd_chars onto the active trace. Filter by mode in two clicks, compare confidence distributions across model versions, spot regressions early.

obs_bullets.title: Fail-fast at the boundary
obs_bullets.desc: Agent errors surface as 502 with the full traceback in logs. Workflow state inconsistencies (missing FitVerdict, missing OutreachDraft) surface as 500 with actionable detail. Nothing is silently swallowed.

career_copilot_page.frontend_intro: Next.js 15 with the App Router. React 19 with the new use() and Server Component conventions. Tailwind v4 + Radix primitives in a shadcn-style component library.

frontend_bullets.title: TanStack Query for async UX
frontend_bullets.desc: Mutations for /analyze, file upload UX with mutation state. Sonner toasts surface backend errors with the structured 502 / 422 messages.

frontend_bullets.title: Zod-validated forms
frontend_bullets.desc: Both modes share a single form schema. Client-side validation prevents most 4xx round-trips before they reach the network.

frontend_bullets.title: PDF upload + paste
frontend_bullets.desc: Two input modes per side (CV / JD): paste text or upload PDF. Upload posts to /v1/extract-pdf, response feeds the same /analyze call.

career_copilot_page.stack_intro: Modern Python and TypeScript across the board. Every choice is one I would make again on a fresh greenfield project.
career_copilot_page.cta_title: Try it on your own CV and JD
career_copilot_page.cta_desc: Paste a CV, paste a job description, pick a mode. Get a verdict and a draft in seconds.
